title: Public key file option documentation broken
body:
The documentation page says:
**--public-key-file public key file
File containing the public keys to use when verifying signatures. If this is not specified, keys from the GPG homedir are used.**

Skopeo says no!
```
$ skopeo standalone-verify --public-key-file
FATA[0000] unknown flag: --public-key-file
$ rpm -qi skopeo
Name        : skopeo
Epoch       : 2
Version     : 1.9.4
...

```

It is without the double hyphen like this (sorry not a working example): 
```
$ skopeo standalone-verify public-key-file
FATA[0000] Usage: skopeo standalone-verify manifest docker-reference key-fingerprint signature
```
ref comments: (show resolved) if interested,
https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1950/files#discussion_r1152658274
 
further: man page says nothing about this!?

Love your work, and this seems trivial - until it drives you nuts wondering why it doesn't work!

comments.body:
Where are you finding this "documentation page"? Is it possible that you're reading online documentation? If so, that's your problem: the only valid documentation for the version you have installed is the man page on that system.

In any case, you seem to be running `1.9.4`, and the `--public-key-file` option was added in the PR you linked to, which was then released in `1.12`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Assuming that this is indeed explained by running an older version of Skopeo, please reopen if that’s not the case.
